subparts.text: What is a project? Discuss its key characteristics that distinguish it from routine operations. 1
What are the critical factors that influence the selection of a project site? 2
What is secondary information in the context of market analysis? 2
How does the discounted cash flow method address some of the limitations of the payback period in project evaluation? 1
Describe the different phases of a project life cycle. How do these phases contribute to the successful execution of a project? 1
What is the significance of market analysis during project formulation? How would you characterize a market, in terms of demand and customer segmentation? 1

Conduct a discounted cash flow calculation to determine the NPV of the following project, assuming a required rate of return of 0.2. The project will cost $75,000 but will result in cash inflows of $20,000, $25,000, $30,000, and $50,000 in each of the next four years. 1
What are the aspects considered for technical analysis of a typical project? Discuss three important issues that managers must consider while conducting technical analysis. 2

Two new Internet site projects are proposed to a young start-up company. Project A will cost $250,000 to implement and is expected to have annual net cash flows of $75,000. Project B will cost $150,000 to implement and should generate annual net cash flows of $52,000. Considering the company's concern about cash flow, determine which project is preferable using the payback period method. Justify your choice. 2
Explain any three demand forecasting methods commonly used in project management. How do these methods assist in making informed project decisions? 2
